close_reason: Already fixed in commit 77368b2: replaced deprecated load_from_env with defaults().load()
description:
secretx-aws-sm/src/lib.rs:84 calls 'aws_config::load_from_env().await'. In aws-config 1.8, this function is annotated:

    #[cfg(not(feature = "behavior-version-latest"))]
    #[deprecated(note = "Use the 'aws_config::load_defaults' function. ...")]
    pub async fn load_from_env() -> SdkConfig { ... }

The workspace Cargo.toml:53 sets 'aws-config = { version = "1", features = ["behavior-version-latest"] }', which suppresses the deprecation warning by gating out the deprecated definition entirely — so the workspace currently compiles clean.

Why this is still a finding:

1. Coupling. The current call site only compiles because the workspace feature is set. If a downstream crate ever depends on secretx-aws-sm without that feature (or aws-config drops the feature gate in 2.x and removes load_from_env), the call site breaks.

2. Explicit intent. The non-deprecated form makes the behavior-version pin visible at the call site:
       let config = aws_config::defaults(aws_config::BehaviorVersion::latest()).load().await;
   That documents 'we accept future default-behavior changes' rather than hiding it in a workspace feature flag.

3. The aws-config docs (lib.rs:32-40, 93) explicitly recommend the 'defaults(BehaviorVersion)' form in their own examples.

Fix:

    let config = aws_config::defaults(aws_config::BehaviorVersion::latest()).load().await;
    Ok(aws_sdk_secretsmanager::Client::new(&config))

Also update the docstring at lib.rs:37 which mentions 'aws_config::load_from_env'.

Note: secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:88 and secretx-aws-ssm/src/lib.rs:51 likely have the same call. Flag in their own passes.
title: secretx-aws-sm: aws_config::load_from_env() is #[deprecated]; use aws_config::defaults(BehaviorVersion::latest())
